primary_error.message: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Missing loop invariants prevent Dafny from proving postconditions about the Two Sum algorithm's correctness.

problem_details.what: The while loop lacks invariants to describe: (1) the relationship between map keys/values and array indices, (2) the mathematical property that map entries encode (target - nums[j] maps to j), (3) that all previously examined pairs don't sum to target, and (4) the InMap predicate property for processed elements.
problem_details.why: The Two Sum algorithm uses a hash map to store complements: for each index j, it stores (target - nums[j]) -> j. When finding nums[i] in the map, it means there exists some earlier index j where target - nums[j] == nums[i], thus nums[j] + nums[i] == target. Without invariants explaining this relationship, Dafny cannot verify that: (1) m[nums[i]] is a valid index less than i, (2) the sum property holds, (3) this is the first valid pair, and (4) returning (-1,-1) means no solution exists.
problem_details.how: Add five loop invariants: (1) 'invariant i <= nums.Length' for index bounds, (2) 'invariant forall k :: k in m ==> 0 <= m[k] < i' to ensure map values are valid indices less than current position, (3) 'invariant forall k :: k in m ==> nums[m[k]] + k == target' to encode the complement relationship, (4) 'invariant InMap(nums[..i], m, target)' to track that all processed elements have their complements in the map, (5) 'invariant forall u, v :: 0 <= u < v < i ==> nums[u] + nums[v] != target' to establish that no valid pair was found in the examined portion.
